component_identification.content:
#### Books & Boards
- 1 rulebook, 1 appendix (bird powers reference with all 170 bird abilities)
- 1 goal board (double-sided: Green competitive / Blue independent)
- 1 bird tray (holds 3 face-up bird cards for open drafting)
- 1 scorepad
- 5 player mats (3 habitat rows: Forest, Grassland, Wetland — each with 5 bird slots)

#### Cards
- 170 unique bird cards — each shows: habitat icon(s), food cost, nest type, egg capacity, wingspan measurement, point value, and a power with timing indicator
- 26 bonus cards (secret end-game scoring objectives — each player keeps 1)
- 8 goal tiles (4 placed randomly on goal board each game, double-sided for variety)

#### Tokens & Dice
- 103 food tokens in 5 types: invertebrate (orange), seed (gold), fish (teal), fruit (pink), rodent (grey)
- 75 egg miniatures in 5 colors (color is cosmetic — no gameplay effect)
- 40 wooden action cubes (8 per player in matching colors)
- 5 custom food dice (faces show single food icons or split-face combinations)

#### Other Components
- 1 birdfeeder dice tower (cardboard construction — holds and rolls food dice, serves as the shared food supply)
- 1 first-player token

#### Player Mat Layout
Each player mat has 3 rows (habitats) and 5 columns. The leftmost column slots are always exposed; columns 2–5 are covered until birds are placed. Each habitat row shows increasing action values from left to right — placing more birds in a row exposes stronger action slots.

core_game_loop.content:
#### Game Structure
Wingspan lasts exactly 4 rounds. Each round, players take turns placing action cubes to perform actions. Available actions shrink each round: 8 turns in Round 1, then 7, 6, and 5 (one cube is spent each round to mark end-of-round goal scoring).

#### Turn Flow
On your turn, choose 1 of 4 actions:
1. **Play a Bird** — Pay its food cost (and egg cost if placing in columns 2–5), place it in a matching habitat row. Resolve any WHEN PLAYED power.
2. **Gain Food (Forest row)** — Place cube on leftmost open Forest slot. Take food dice from the birdfeeder matching the slot value. Then activate brown powers right-to-left across your Forest birds.
3. **Lay Eggs (Grassland row)** — Place cube on leftmost open Grassland slot. Lay eggs on your birds (up to each bird's egg limit) equal to the slot value. Then activate brown powers right-to-left.
4. **Draw Bird Cards (Wetland row)** — Place cube on leftmost open Wetland slot. Draw cards from the deck or face-up tray per slot value. Then activate brown powers right-to-left.

#### Engine-Building Pattern
More birds in a habitat row means more brown powers activate when you use that row. A Forest with 3 birds gives you the slot benefit plus 3 brown-power activations — this is how engines compound.

#### Between Rounds
1. Remove all action cubes from player mats.
2. Score the current round-end goal — each player places 1 action cube on the goal board to mark their score. This cube is permanently spent (reducing next round's available actions).
3. Discard all face-up bird cards from the tray and refill with 3 new cards from the deck.
4. Pass the first-player token clockwise.

#### Player Interaction
Wingspan is primarily a parallel engine-building game. Direct interaction comes from: competing for face-up bird cards in the tray, competing for food dice in the birdfeeder, competing for end-of-round goal placements (Green side), and pink Between Turns powers that trigger off opponents' actions.

detailed_rules.content:
#### Playing a Bird
- **Habitat restriction** — Each bird card shows which habitat(s) it can be placed in (Forest, Grassland, Wetland, or a combination).
- **Food cost** — Pay the food tokens shown on the bird card. A wild icon can be paid with any food type. A crossed-out circle means no food cost.
- **2-for-1 conversion** — When playing a bird only, you may trade any 2 food tokens for 1 food of any type you need.
- **Egg cost by column** — Column 1: free. Columns 2–3: 1 egg. Columns 4–5: 2 eggs. Eggs are removed from any bird(s) you own.
- **Placement** — Always place on the leftmost open slot in the chosen habitat.

#### Bird Power Types
- **WHEN PLAYED (white banner)** — Triggers once, immediately when the bird is placed. Example: gain food from the supply, draw extra cards, or tuck a card.
- **WHEN ACTIVATED / Brown power (brown banner)** — Triggers every time you take the habitat action for that row. Powers resolve right-to-left. These are the engine-building core. Example: Common Raven — discard 1 egg from this bird to gain 1 food from the supply. Franklin's Gull — discard 1 egg to draw 2 bird cards.
- **ONCE BETWEEN TURNS / Pink power (pink banner)** — May trigger on other players' turns when specified conditions are met. Example: when another player takes the Gain Food action, you may cache 1 food from the supply on this bird.
- **ROUND END (no banner / end-of-round)** — Triggers once at the end of each round. Example: score bonus points based on birds in a specific habitat.
- **Brown powers are optional** unless card text says otherwise. Pink powers are always optional.

#### Birdfeeder Management
- The birdfeeder dice tower holds 5 custom dice showing food icons.
- When gaining food, take dice from the feeder matching your slot value. Each die gives 1 food token matching its face. Split-face dice: choose one icon, not both.
- **Empty feeder** — If the feeder is empty when you need to take food, reroll all 5 dice.
- **All same face** — If all remaining dice show the same face, you may reroll all 5 dice before taking food.
- **Card-to-food trade** — Some Forest slots allow discarding 1 bird card from hand to gain 1 additional food from remaining dice.

#### Egg Rules
- Eggs are laid on birds you own, up to each bird's printed egg capacity.
- Egg color is cosmetic — no gameplay effect.
- Eggs beyond capacity are lost.
- Eggs serve two purposes: currency to play birds in columns 2–5, and end-game victory points (1 VP each).

#### Cached Food and Tucked Cards
- Some bird powers let you **cache** food tokens on a bird card. Cached food counts as 1 VP each at game end but cannot be spent as regular food.
- Some bird powers let you **tuck** bird cards from your hand (or the deck) behind a bird. Tucked cards count as 1 VP each at game end.

#### Nest Types
- Birds have one of 5 nest types: Platform, Bowl, Cavity, Ground, or Wild (star icon).
- Nest type matters for bonus cards and some round-end goals. Wild nests count as any nest type for scoring purposes.

#### Goal Board Modes
- **Green side (competitive)** — Players compete for 1st/2nd/3rd place on each round's goal. Tie-break: add tied-place points plus the next place(s) below, divide by number of tied players, round down. 4th/5th place players still place a cube but score 0.
- **Blue side (independent)** — Each qualifying item earns 1 point, up to a maximum shown on the goal.

#### Bonus Cards
- Each player keeps 1 bonus card during setup (from 2 dealt). Bonus cards award end-game points for meeting specific conditions (e.g., birds in a specific habitat, birds with specific nest types, birds from a geographic region).

#### Setup Summary
1. Shuffle all 170 bird cards into one deck. Reveal 3 face-up into the bird tray.
2. Place all food and egg tokens in shared supply.
3. Toss all 5 dice into the birdfeeder dice tower.
4. Choose Green or Blue side of the goal board. Place 1 random goal tile on each of the 4 round spaces.
5. Shuffle bonus cards into a deck.
6. Each player takes: 1 player mat, 8 action cubes, 5 random bird cards, 2 random bonus cards, and 5 food tokens (1 of each type).
7. Each player secretly decides how many birds to keep (0–5). For each bird kept, discard 1 food token. Keep 1 of 2 bonus cards; discard the other.
8. Randomly select first player.

#### Solo Mode (Automa)
- Uses an Automa deck that simulates an opponent. The Automa takes actions via card draws rather than strategic decisions. Your goal is to outscore the Automa's programmed scoring over 4 rounds.

scoring_and_endgame.content:
#### Game End
The game ends after all players have completed Round 4 (using their final 5 action cubes).

#### All 7 Victory Point Sources
1. **Bird card points** — Each bird in your tableau has a printed point value (0–9). Sum all of them.
2. **Bonus card points** — Score your kept bonus card(s) based on how well your tableau meets the card's condition.
3. **End-of-round goal points** — Points earned from the 4 round-end goals scored during the game.
4. **Eggs** — 1 VP per egg on your birds at game end.
5. **Cached food** — 1 VP per food token cached on bird cards.
6. **Tucked cards** — 1 VP per card tucked behind bird cards.
7. **Nectar points (Oceania expansion)** — If playing with Oceania expansion, nectar placed in habitats scores end-of-round majority bonuses. In base game, this source does not apply.

#### Scoring Tips
- Bird card points are usually the largest category (30–50% of total score).
- Eggs and tucked cards are the easiest points to generate in bulk during Rounds 3–4.
- Cached food points are opportunistic — take them when bird powers offer them but don't build around them.
- End-of-round goals are worth 0–5 points per round depending on placement (Green side) or count (Blue side).
- Bonus cards typically contribute 5–10 points. Strong bonus card alignment can swing a close game.

#### Typical Winning Scores
- 2-player: 75–95 points
- 3-4 player: 80–100 points
- Experienced play: 90–120+ points

#### Using the Scorepad
The scorepad has columns for each VP source. At game end, total each source separately, then sum all columns. This helps identify where your strategy succeeded or fell short.

#### Tie-Breaking
- Primary: highest total points wins.
- If tied: player with the most unused food tokens wins.
- If still tied: shared victory.

beginner_strategy.content:
#### Opening Hand Strategy
- **Keep 1–2 birds, maximize food.** For each bird you keep, you discard 1 food token. Keeping 4–5 birds leaves you food-starved and unable to play anything for several turns. Ideal: keep 1 cheap bird (1 food cost) and 4 food tokens so you can play a bird on turn 1.
- **Prioritize brown-power birds in your opening.** A brown-power bird played on turn 1 may activate 6–8 times across the game. The same bird played on turn 12 activates once.
- **Play a Forest bird first if possible.** Early food generation lets you afford more birds sooner, snowballing your engine.

#### The Power 4 — Strongest Base Game Cards
If you see any of these in your opening hand or the bird tray, take them:
- **Common Raven / Chihuahuan Raven** — Brown power: discard 1 egg to gain 1 food. In Grasslands, every Lay Eggs action also becomes a Gain Food action. These birds are so strong the rulebook acknowledges they can be removed for balance.
- **Franklin's Gull** (3 VP, wild nest) — Brown power: discard 1 egg to draw 2 cards. Provides massive card flow.
- **Killdeer** (1 VP) — Same power as Franklin's Gull. Lower points but equally efficient as an engine piece.
Place all four in Grasslands for a self-sustaining engine that converts eggs into food and cards simultaneously.

#### Key Bird Synergies
- **Ravens + Tucking Birds** (Barn Swallow, Cedar Waxwing, Dark-Eyed Junco): Ravens convert eggs to food, tucking birds convert cards to points. In a Grassland row together, one Lay Eggs action generates eggs (1 VP each), food (via Ravens), and tucked-card points.
- **Northern Mockingbird + Any Strong Brown Power**: Mockingbird copies the brown power of another bird in its habitat. Pair with a Raven and you double your egg-to-food conversion every activation.
- **Play-Another-Bird powers** (Yellowhammer, Magpie Lark) + high-point zero-food birds (Bonelli's Eagle, Eastern Imperial Eagle): Drop expensive scoring birds onto your board without spending food or an action — massive tempo advantage in Rounds 3–4.

#### Habitat Tempo
- **Rounds 1–2: Forest focus.** Get food generation online. Even 1–2 cheap Forest birds dramatically boost your food income.
- **Rounds 2–3: Wetland support.** Card draw keeps your options open and feeds tucking engines.
- **Rounds 3–4: Grassland push.** Eggs are the most reliable point source. Three eggs per turn in Round 4 is 3+ easy points per action.
- **Focus on 1–2 habitats**, not all three. Spreading equally dilutes your engine.

#### Common First-Game Mistakes
- **Action ping-pong** — Rotating all four actions evenly instead of repeating your highest-value habitat row.
- **Ignoring the shrinking action economy** — You lose 1 action per round (8→7→6→5). Your engine must be built by Round 3 so Round 4 actions purely score points.
- **Hoarding cards in hand** — Cards in hand score zero. They only matter when played to your board or tucked behind birds.
- **Chasing bonus cards too hard** — Bonus cards typically yield 5–7 points in an 80+ point game. Use them to break decision ties, not as your primary strategy.
- **Over-relying on pink (Between Turns) powers** — Pink powers depend on opponents' choices and are unreliable. Brown powers that you control are always more dependable.

#### Round-End Goals
- Don't overcommit. The point spread between 1st and 3rd place is typically 2–4 points. If chasing a goal would derail your engine, skip it.
- Favor goals that align with your existing strategy rather than pivoting mid-game.
- Wild-nest birds count toward any nest-type goal — prioritize them when nest goals are on the board.

walkthrough.content:
#### Your First Game of Wingspan — A Guided Walkthrough

**Setup**
Each player gets a player mat, 8 action cubes, 5 random bird cards, 2 bonus cards, and 5 food tokens (one of each type). Now comes your first decision:

**The Keep/Discard Decision**
For each bird card you keep, discard 1 food token. Look at your birds:
- Do any cost only 1 food? Keep that one — you can play it turn 1.
- Do any have brown powers? Those are engine pieces — prioritize keeping them.
- Is any bird 5+ points? Worth keeping for late-game play.
Recommendation: Keep 1-2 birds, discard the rest. You want 3-4 food tokens to start. Keep 1 of your 2 bonus cards — whichever seems easier to achieve.

**Round 1 (8 Turns)**

*Turn 1 — Play a Bird*: Place your cheapest bird in the Forest row (if it's a Forest bird). Pay its food cost. If it has a WHEN PLAYED power, resolve it now. You now have a bird generating food every time you use the Forest action!

*Turn 2 — Gain Food (Forest)*: Place your action cube on the leftmost open Forest slot. Take food from the birdfeeder dice tower. If your bird has a brown power, activate it now. You're earning food AND getting a bonus effect!

*Turns 3-4*: Either play another bird (if you can afford one) or keep gaining food/drawing cards to prepare. If a great bird appears in the face-up tray, draw cards from the Wetland action.

*Turns 5-8*: Keep alternating between playing birds and using your habitat rows. By end of Round 1, aim to have 3-4 birds placed.

**End of Round 1**
Score the first round-end goal. Place 1 action cube on the goal board — this cube is gone forever, so you'll have only 7 turns in Round 2.

**Round 2 (7 Turns)**
Your engine should start producing. Focus on:
- Playing birds with strong brown powers in your focused habitat(s)
- Using your habitat actions to generate resources AND trigger brown powers
- Starting to lay eggs (Grassland) — eggs are 1 VP each at game end

**Round 3 (6 Turns)**
Engine should be humming. Every action should generate multiple benefits:
- Gain Food in a Forest with 3 birds = food from the slot + 3 brown power activations
- Lay Eggs in a Grassland with 3 birds = eggs + 3 brown power activations
- Play high-VP birds with the resources your engine produces

**Round 4 (5 Turns)**
Last round — purely scoring actions:
- Lay eggs as much as possible (most reliable VP per action)
- Play any remaining high-VP birds from your hand
- Don't waste turns on setup — everything should score now

**Scoring**
Use the scorepad. Add up: bird card points + bonus card + round-end goals + eggs + cached food + tucked cards. First game target: 60-80 points is great. 80+ is excellent.

advanced_strategy.content:
#### The Three Meta-Strategies

Expert Wingspan play revolves around three macro strategies. Most winning games blend two of them:

**1. Big Bird Points**
Focus on playing many high-VP birds (5-9 point cards). Use the Forest for food generation and the Wetland for card draw to find and afford expensive birds.
- Strength: Consistent, hard to disrupt
- Weakness: Slow to build, fewer engine activations
- Target: 8-12 birds placed, 40-60 bird card points
- Best when: You draw multiple 6+ point birds early

**2. Tucking/Caching Engine**
Build a Grassland engine around tucking birds (Barn Swallow, Cedar Waxwing) and egg-to-resource converters (Ravens). Each Lay Eggs action generates eggs (VP), food (via Ravens), and tucked cards (VP).
- Strength: Explosive compound scoring in Rounds 3-4
- Weakness: Needs specific birds to come together; slow start
- Target: 15-25 tucked cards, 8-12 eggs, 30+ bird points
- Best when: You find Ravens + tucking birds in Rounds 1-2

**3. Egg Spam**
Maximize Grassland row for egg production. Fill Grassland with high-egg-capacity birds and repeat Lay Eggs action every turn in Rounds 3-4.
- Strength: Simplest to execute, less card-dependent
- Weakness: Lower ceiling than tucking engine
- Target: 15-20 eggs, decent bird points
- Best when: Your draws don't support a specific engine

#### Advanced Engine Patterns

**The Grassland Mega-Engine**
Ravens + Tucking Birds + Egg-Layers in Grassland. One Lay Eggs action:
1. Lay 2-3 eggs (2-3 VP)
2. Ravens convert eggs → food
3. Tucking birds convert cards → tucked VP
4. Result: 4-6 VP per single action. Repeat 3-5 times in Round 4.

**The Forest Food Chain**
Birds that cache food (Pink-footed Goose, Black-Bellied Whistling-Duck) + food-to-card converters. Each Gain Food action generates food + cached VP + cards.

**Play-Another-Bird Chains**
Yellowhopper/Magpie Lark in a habitat with expensive high-VP birds. When activated, they let you play a bird for free — massive tempo. A 7-point bird played for free is a 7 VP swing.

**Mockingbird Doubling**
Northern Mockingbird copies any other bird's brown power in the same habitat. Place it next to your strongest engine bird to double its output.

#### Power Type Analysis

**Brown (When Activated) — The Core**
- Most valuable because YOU control when they trigger
- Play brown-power birds early for maximum activations
- A brown power bird played Round 1 may activate 15+ times. Same bird Round 4: 2-3 times.

**Pink (Between Turns) — Bonus Income**
- Trigger on opponents' turns, so frequency depends on player count and opponent actions
- In 4-5 player games, pink powers trigger often and are strong
- In 2-player games, pink powers are unreliable
- Best pink powers: cache food or tuck cards when opponents take specific actions

**White (When Played) — Tempo Plays**
- One-time effects, so they don't compound
- Best on expensive late-game birds (free resources when you need them)
- "Play another bird" when-played powers are the best white powers in the game

**Round End Powers**
- Trigger once per round (4 times max)
- Low-frequency but can be impactful if they score VP directly

#### Round-by-Round Expert Tempo

**Round 1 (8 actions):** Play 2-3 birds, focus on Forest for food. At least 1 brown-power bird in your primary habitat. Don't waste actions drawing cards unless you need specific engine pieces.

**Round 2 (7 actions):** Complete your engine core. Play 2-3 more birds. Start using habitat actions that trigger multiple brown powers. Begin laying eggs if your Grassland has 2+ birds.

**Round 3 (6 actions):** Shift to scoring. Play 1-2 high-VP birds, otherwise spam your best habitat row repeatedly. Egg production should be reliable.

**Round 4 (5 actions):** Pure scoring. Every action should maximize VP. Typically: Lay Eggs 3-4 times (generating eggs + brown power VP), play 1 high-VP bird if affordable. DO NOT spend actions drawing cards in Round 4.

#### Player Count Adjustments

**2 Players:**
- Birdfeeder competition is minimal — you can usually get the food you need
- Pink powers are weak (only 1 opponent to trigger them)
- Card tray competition is low — you can plan around face-up birds
- Games are more engine-focused, less interactive

**3-4 Players:**
- Balanced experience. Birdfeeder and card tray have moderate competition.
- Pink powers are decent — trigger 2-3 times per round
- End-of-round goals (Green side) become more competitive

**5 Players:**
- Birdfeeder empties frequently — rerolls happen often
- Pink powers are very strong (4 opponents = 4 chances to trigger)
- Card tray turns over rapidly — don't plan around specific face-up birds
- End-of-round goals are fiercely contested

#### Advanced Bonus Card Play

- Most bonus cards score 5-8 VP. Don't warp your strategy for a bonus card unless it's already aligned.
- **Geography bonuses** (birds from a specific region) are the hardest to score — too dependent on card draw
- **Nest type bonuses** are moderate — Wild nests count as any type
- **Habitat bonuses** (birds in a specific habitat) are the easiest — you're already focusing habitats
- If your bonus card and round-end goals pull in different directions, ignore the bonus card. Round goals are scored throughout the game; bonus is just once.

#### Rules Edge Cases

- **"May" vs "Must"**: Brown powers that say "may" are optional. Powers that don't say "may" are mandatory. Read carefully.
- **Pink power timing**: Pink powers trigger on other players' turns, but you must pay attention and announce them. If the moment passes, you lose the trigger.
- **Birdfeeder reroll**: You can reroll all dice when the feeder is empty OR when all remaining dice show the same face. Check before taking food.
- **Egg capacity**: You cannot place eggs on a bird beyond its printed capacity. Excess eggs from Lay Eggs actions are simply lost.
- **2-for-1 food conversion**: This ONLY applies when playing a bird. You cannot use it during the Gain Food action.
- **Tucking from deck vs hand**: Some tuck powers say "from hand" and others say "from the deck." Deck-tucking is strictly better because it doesn't cost a hand card.

metadata.notes: Content overhaul — enhanced strategy tab with card tier lists, engine archetypes, action economy framework. Added rules_citations with 10 key rulings. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG official rulings list, Wingsplain strategy guides, r/wingspan.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Books & Boards
- 1 rulebook, 1 appendix (bird powers reference with all 170 bird abilities)
- 1 goal board (double-sided: Green competitive / Blue independent)
- 1 bird tray (holds 3 face-up bird cards for open drafting)
- 1 scorepad
- 5 player mats (3 habitat rows: Forest, Grassland, Wetland — each with 5 bird slots)

#### Cards
- 170 unique bird cards — each shows: habitat icon(s), food cost, nest type, egg capacity, wingspan measurement, point value, and a power with timing indicator
- 26 bonus cards (secret end-game scoring objectives — each player keeps 1)
- 8 goal tiles (4 placed randomly on goal board each game, double-sided for variety)

#### Tokens & Dice
- 103 food tokens in 5 types: invertebrate (orange), seed (gold), fish (teal), fruit (pink), rodent (grey)
- 75 egg miniatures in 5 colors (color is cosmetic — no gameplay effect)
- 40 wooden action cubes (8 per player in matching colors)
- 5 custom food dice (faces show single food icons or split-face combinations)

#### Other Components
- 1 birdfeeder dice tower (cardboard construction — holds and rolls food dice, serves as the shared food supply)
- 1 first-player token

#### Player Mat Layout
Each player mat has 3 rows (habitats) and 5 columns. The leftmost column slots are always exposed; columns 2–5 are covered until birds are placed. Each habitat row shows increasing action values from left to right — placing more birds in a row exposes stronger action slots.

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Game Structure
Wingspan lasts exactly 4 rounds. Each round, players take turns placing action cubes to perform actions. Available actions shrink each round: 8 turns in Round 1, then 7, 6, and 5 (one cube is spent each round to mark end-of-round goal scoring).

#### Turn Flow
On your turn, choose 1 of 4 actions:
1. **Play a Bird** — Pay its food cost (and egg cost if placing in columns 2–5), place it in a matching habitat row. Resolve any WHEN PLAYED power.
2. **Gain Food (Forest row)** — Place cube on leftmost open Forest slot. Take food dice from the birdfeeder matching the slot value. Then activate brown powers right-to-left across your Forest birds.
3. **Lay Eggs (Grassland row)** — Place cube on leftmost open Grassland slot. Lay eggs on your birds (up to each bird's egg limit) equal to the slot value. Then activate brown powers right-to-left.
4. **Draw Bird Cards (Wetland row)** — Place cube on leftmost open Wetland slot. Draw cards from the deck or face-up tray per slot value. Then activate brown powers right-to-left.

#### Engine-Building Pattern
More birds in a habitat row means more brown powers activate when you use that row. A Forest with 3 birds gives you the slot benefit plus 3 brown-power activations — this is how engines compound.

#### Between Rounds
1. Remove all action cubes from player mats.
2. Score the current round-end goal — each player places 1 action cube on the goal board to mark their score. This cube is permanently spent (reducing next round's available actions).
3. Discard all face-up bird cards from the tray and refill with 3 new cards from the deck.
4. Pass the first-player token clockwise.

#### Player Interaction
Wingspan is primarily a parallel engine-building game. Direct interaction comes from: competing for face-up bird cards in the tray, competing for food dice in the birdfeeder, competing for end-of-round goal placements (Green side), and pink Between Turns powers that trigger off opponents' actions.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Playing a Bird
- **Habitat restriction** — Each bird card shows which habitat(s) it can be placed in (Forest, Grassland, Wetland, or a combination).
- **Food cost** — Pay the food tokens shown on the bird card. A wild icon can be paid with any food type. A crossed-out circle means no food cost.
- **2-for-1 conversion** — When playing a bird only, you may trade any 2 food tokens for 1 food of any type you need.
- **Egg cost by column** — Column 1: free. Columns 2–3: 1 egg. Columns 4–5: 2 eggs. Eggs are removed from any bird(s) you own.
- **Placement** — Always place on the leftmost open slot in the chosen habitat.

#### Bird Power Types
- **WHEN PLAYED (white banner)** — Triggers once, immediately when the bird is placed. Example: gain food from the supply, draw extra cards, or tuck a card.
- **WHEN ACTIVATED / Brown power (brown banner)** — Triggers every time you take the habitat action for that row. Powers resolve right-to-left. These are the engine-building core.
- **ONCE BETWEEN TURNS / Pink power (pink banner)** — May trigger on other players' turns when specified conditions are met.
- **ROUND END (no banner / end-of-round)** — Triggers once at the end of each round.
- **Brown powers are optional** unless card text says otherwise. Pink powers are always optional.

#### Birdfeeder Management
- The birdfeeder holds 5 custom dice.
- Take dice matching your slot value. Split-face dice: choose one icon, not both.
- **Empty feeder** — Reroll all 5 dice.
- **All same face** — You may reroll all 5 dice before taking food.
- **Card-to-food trade** — Some Forest slots allow discarding 1 card for 1 additional food.

#### Egg Rules
- Eggs laid on birds up to each bird's printed capacity. Color is cosmetic.
- Eggs = currency for columns 2-5 AND 1 VP each at game end.

#### Cached Food and Tucked Cards
- **Cached food**: 1 VP each at game end, cannot be spent as regular food.
- **Tucked cards**: 1 VP each at game end.

#### Nest Types
5 types: Platform, Bowl, Cavity, Ground, Wild (star). Wild counts as any type for scoring.

#### Goal Board
- **Green (competitive)**: 1st/2nd/3rd place scoring. Ties: divide summed places by number tied, round down.
- **Blue (independent)**: 1 point per qualifying item up to max.

#### Bonus Cards
Keep 1 from 2 dealt. End-game VP for meeting conditions (habitat count, nest types, regions, etc.).

#### Setup
1. Shuffle 170 bird cards, reveal 3 face-up. 2. Food/egg supply ready. 3. Toss 5 dice in birdfeeder. 4. Goal board + 4 random goal tiles. 5. Each player: mat, 8 cubes, 5 birds, 2 bonus cards, 5 food (1 each). 6. Keep 0-5 birds (discard 1 food per bird kept). Keep 1 bonus card. 7. Random first player.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
After all players complete Round 4.

#### 7 VP Sources
1. **Bird card points** (0-9 each)
2. **Bonus card points**
3. **End-of-round goal points**
4. **Eggs** (1 VP each)
5. **Cached food** (1 VP each)
6. **Tucked cards** (1 VP each)
7. **Nectar** (Oceania expansion only)

#### Typical Scores
- 2-player: 75-95 | 3-4 player: 80-100 | Expert: 90-120+

#### Tiebreaker
Most unused food tokens. If still tied: shared victory.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Hand Strategy
- **Keep 1–2 birds, maximize food.** Keeping 4-5 birds leaves you food-starved. Ideal: keep 1 cheap bird + 4 food tokens.
- **Prioritize brown-power birds.** A brown power played turn 1 may activate 15+ times across the game. The same bird played turn 20 activates once.
- **Play a Forest bird first if possible.** Early food generation snowballs your engine — aim for 2 food per Gain Food action by mid-Round 1.

#### The Power 4 — Game-Defining Cards
These four cards warp games around them. If you see them, take them:
- **Chihuahuan Raven / Common Raven** — Discard 1 egg for 2 wild food. In Grasslands, every Lay Eggs action also becomes a Gain Food action. Self-sustaining engine.
- **Franklin's Gull / Killdeer** — Discard 1 egg to draw 2 cards. Massive card flow that feeds tucking engines.
- **Optimal sequence**: Play Killdeer first in Grasslands → lay eggs to activate draw → gain food → play Raven in same habitat → continuous cycles of eggs, food, and cards.

#### Card Tier Guide
- **God Tier**: Bonelli's Eagle, Eastern Imperial Eagle — tuck-to-play birds that generate 9-11 VP per activation by playing free high-point birds.
- **Tier 0**: Black Redstart, Lesser Whitethroat (mass egg layers), Galah, Noisy Miner, Maned Duck — game-shaping engines.
- **Tier 1**: Osprey, Common Swift, Ruff, American Crow, Anna's Hummingbird — efficient and effective.
- **Predators are generally weak** — dice-rolling predators succeed at most 50% of the time. Avoid as primary strategy.

#### Three Scoring Engines

**1. Tucking Engine (Highest Ceiling)**
Build around tucking birds: Barn Swallow, House Finch, Purple Martin, Red-Winged Blackbird. Each tucked card = 1 VP. Tuck/lay birds (tuck then lay egg) generate 2+ VP per activation. Maned Duck paired with tuck/lay birds outperforms Raven-based engines.

**2. Egg Spam (Most Reliable)**
Fill Grassland with high-egg-capacity birds (Bobolink, Inca Dove, Malleefowl). Repeat Lay Eggs in Rounds 3-4. Simple, consistent, but lower ceiling than tucking.

**3. Big Bird Points (Most Consistent)**
Play many high-VP birds (5-9 points). Forest for food, Wetland for draws. Target: 40-60 bird card points. Less card-dependent than engine strategies.

#### Key Synergies
- **Ravens + Tucking Birds**: Lay Eggs generates eggs + food (via Ravens) + tucked VP. The Grassland mega-engine.
- **Mockingbird + Any Strong Brown Power**: Doubles your best engine bird's output in the same habitat.
- **Play-Another-Bird + High-VP Birds**: Yellowhammer, Moltoni's Warbler (European) let you play a bird for free — a 7-point bird played for free is a 7 VP swing.
- **Galah + Tucking Birds**: Galah generates food AND points alongside tucking.

#### Habitat Tempo
- **Rounds 1-2**: Forest focus (food engine). Even 1-2 cheap Forest birds boost income dramatically.
- **Rounds 2-3**: Wetland support (card flow feeds tucking/playing). Aim for 2 cards per Draw action.
- **Rounds 3-4**: Grassland push (egg VP + engine finisher). Eggs are the most reliable VP per action.
- **Focus 1-2 habitats**, not all three. Spreading equally dilutes your engine.

#### Action Economy
You get exactly **26 activations** across the game (8+7+6+5). Every action must count.
- Continuous mid-level activation outscores waiting for perfect plays.
- Round 4: pure scoring. Lay eggs 3-4 times. NO card drawing in Round 4.
- A big-point bird play is often better than repeatedly activating a low-point engine.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Action ping-pong** — Rotating all 4 actions instead of repeating your best habitat row.
- **Ignoring shrinking actions** (8→7→6→5). Engine must be built by Round 3.
- **Hoarding cards** — Cards in hand = 0 VP. Play them or tuck them.
- **Chasing bonus cards** — Typically 5-7 VP in an 80+ game. Use to break ties, not as primary strategy.
- **Over-relying on pink powers** — Opponent-dependent. Strong at 4-5 players, weak at 2.
- **Overdrawing cards** — A fistful of cards without food or tucking powers scores nothing.
- **Sharing resources unwisely** — Don't use shared-resource powers unless you gain substantially more than opponents.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Wingspan

**Setup Decision**: You get 5 bird cards and 5 food tokens. For each bird you keep, discard 1 food. Keep 1-2 cheap birds with brown powers and 3-4 food. Keep whichever bonus card seems easier.

**Round 1 (8 turns)**
*Turn 1*: Play your cheapest bird in the Forest. Pay its food cost. Resolve any WHEN PLAYED power.
*Turn 2*: Gain Food (Forest action). Take food from the birdfeeder. If your bird has a brown power, it activates!
*Turns 3-4*: Play another bird or gain food/draw cards.
*Turns 5-8*: Alternate playing birds and using habitat actions. Aim: 3-4 birds placed.

**End Round 1**: Score the round goal. Place 1 cube on goal board — gone forever. Round 2: 7 turns.

**Round 2 (7 turns)**: Build your engine. Play brown-power birds. Start laying eggs in Grassland.

**Round 3 (6 turns)**: Engine should hum. Each habitat action triggers multiple brown powers. Play high-VP birds.

**Round 4 (5 turns)**: Pure scoring. Lay eggs repeatedly (most reliable VP). Play remaining high-VP birds.

**Score**: Bird points + bonus + goals + eggs + cached food + tucked cards. Target: 60-80 VP first game.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Three Meta-Strategies

**1. Big Bird Points**: Play many high-VP birds (5-9 points). Forest for food, Wetland for draws. Target: 40-60 bird card points. Best when you draw multiple 6+ point birds.

**2. Tucking/Caching Engine**: Grassland engine with Ravens + tucking birds. Each Lay Eggs = eggs + food + tucked VP. Target: 15-25 tucked cards. Best when Ravens + tuckers appear in Rounds 1-2.

**3. Egg Spam**: Fill Grassland with high-capacity birds, repeat Lay Eggs. Simplest, lowest ceiling. Target: 15-20 eggs. Best as fallback.

#### Advanced Engine Patterns
- **Grassland Mega-Engine**: Ravens + tuckers + egg-layers. One Lay Eggs = 4-6 VP.
- **Play-Another-Bird Chains**: Free high-VP bird placement = massive tempo.
- **Mockingbird Doubling**: Copies your best brown power.
- **Forest Food Chain**: Cache food birds + converters.

#### Power Type Analysis
- **Brown**: YOU control timing. Play early for 15+ activations. Core of every engine.
- **Pink**: Opponent-dependent. Strong at 4-5 players, weak at 2 players.
- **White**: One-time. Best on late-game tempo plays.
- **Round End**: Low frequency, moderate value.

#### Expert Tempo
- **R1** (8): Play 2-3 birds, Forest for food. At least 1 brown-power bird.
- **R2** (7): Complete engine. 2-3 more birds. Start eggs if Grassland has 2+ birds.
- **R3** (6): Shift to scoring. Play 1-2 high-VP birds, spam best habitat.
- **R4** (5): Pure scoring. Lay Eggs 3-4 times. NO card drawing in Round 4.

#### Player Count
- **2P**: Low competition. Pink powers weak. Engine-focused.
- **3-4P**: Balanced. Pink decent. Goals contested.
- **5P**: Feeder empties often. Pink strong (4 opponents). Tray turns fast.

#### Bonus Card Play
- Habitat bonuses easiest. Nest type moderate. Geography hardest.
- Don't warp strategy for 5-8 VP. If bonus and goals conflict, ignore bonus.

#### Rules Edge Cases
- "May" = optional. No "may" = mandatory. Read brown powers carefully.
- Pink powers: announce on opponents' turns or lose the trigger.
- Birdfeeder reroll: when empty OR all same face.
- 2-for-1 food: ONLY when playing birds, not during Gain Food.
- Tucking from deck > tucking from hand (no hand card cost).

rulings: Choose food dice one at a time from the birdfeeder — you can trigger a reroll between picks if all remaining dice show the same face after your selection.
Birdfeeder reroll when all dice show the same face is optional (you MAY reroll). An empty birdfeeder reset IS automatic.
The 2-for-1 food substitution (trade 2 food for 1 of any type) ONLY applies when paying bird card costs — not during Gain Food or other effects.
Brown powers activate right-to-left in fixed order across the row. Each activation is optional, but order cannot be changed.
A pink power can fire at most once between each of your turns, regardless of how many opponents trigger it. Pink powers DO activate one final time after your last turn.
Food and egg tokens are unlimited — if physical tokens run out, use substitutes.
Wild (star) nest birds count toward ANY nest-type bonus or end-of-round goal in the base game.
When drawing multiple cards, draw one at a time — you can look at a deck draw, then decide deck or face-up for subsequent draws.
'Repeat a power' birds can copy ANY activated bird in that habitat row (including birds to their right), not just birds to their left.
Players may always decline benefits (lay fewer eggs, gain less food). Costs are mandatory — if you activate a power, you must pay its cost. You CAN choose not to activate entirely.
